title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4 - 15 - 2017: Swam 822 meters, rode 25 miles, ran 8.6 miles

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs a full triathlon training day in Boulder: swimming 822 meters with fins and paddles, cycling 25 miles (averaging 19.2 mph on a 20-column time trial), and running 8.6 miles. He also shares purchases from a bike shop and triathlon store, notes a car sale no-show, and talks about his goal of competing in a local triathlon in August.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: plans to sign up for an August triathlon once he loses weight and builds strength to around 150 lbs
not officially competitive yet — riding a heavy old bike and still under 20 mph average
a dedicated swim bag is worth it because disorganization drains energy needed for training
tight on money and needs to budget carefully as appetite and food costs will increase with heavier training
when there are no plans, treat it as an opportunity for a big training day

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have my shirt off for body transformation and exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I swam 54 laps in a 50-foot pool with fins and paddles.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have no strength in my arm when I push down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to get good at swimming.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tried listening to an audiobook louder this time to see how it works.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Someone was supposed to come look at my car at 3 but never called or showed up.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on my 20-area size page, putting pictures on my arm.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to the bike shop to get gloves because I only had three left gloves and no right.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to the triathlon store called Multi-Sport and got a swim bag, anti-fog goggles, a swim cap, and a triathlon suit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went on a ride and did a 20-column time show at level 61, 19.2 miles per hour, which is faster than last time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I use my website as a tracker for progress.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not officially a micro-racer, but I'm doing bicycle racing with an old 30-pound bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to sign up for a triathlon in August, giving me four months to train.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went running but didn’t go to the top of Mount Sinus because I didn’t have an extra shirt and knew I’d get cold.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I checked out a local running track near a junior high, which is open to anyone and has a water fountain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ran 8.6 miles today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn’t listen to any music or audiobooks during my run.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’m low on money because I gave some away and need to budget carefully.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Organizing and locating things takes too much time and energy, which affects my blood sugar and exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to afford food because my appetite will increase in about three weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have pizza cooking right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow I plan to swim in the morning and possibly ride in the comments.
